frameworks.name: Bayesian inference
frameworks.one_line: Mice estimate block-wise prior probability and combine it with sensory evidence

frameworks.name: brain-wide distributed coding
frameworks.one_line: Prior is encoded across sensory, motor and higher-order regions, not localized

frameworks.name: loop-based neural model
frameworks.one_line: Inter-area loops broadcast the prior versus localizing it to decision-making areas

core_question: Where in the mouse brain is the subjective prior probability over choice options represented?
key_assumptions: Mice can estimate the latent block-wise prior probability
Subjective prior shapes choice accuracy in a Bayesian manner
Neural correlates of the prior can be read out from large-scale recordings
manipulations: Prior probability of stimulus side alternated between 0.2 and 0.8 across blocks
Brain-wide Neuropixels and widefield calcium imaging across regions
key_findings: Mice tracked the prior probability and improved decision accuracy accordingly
Subjective prior was encoded in 20-30% of brain regions sampled
Prior representation spanned early sensory areas (LGN, V1)
Prior representation also spanned motor and high-level cortical regions (M1/M2, dACC, vlOFC)
interpretations: Widespread prior encoding supports a loop-based Bayesian inference architecture
Argues against localized incorporation of the prior only in decision-making areas
